nodes.label: Mittler Senior Technology Hero Background
nodes.description: EXTRACTED: A young male student (likely high school or college age) wearing a blue polo shirt sits at a desk giving a thumbs-up gesture toward an iMac computer screen showing a video call with a grid of participants (Zoom/video conference interface). The room is casual and personal, with sports memorabilia and a bulletin board visible in the background. INFERRED: This image represents a Mittler Senior Technology tutor conducting an online technology class with senior citizens. The energetic thumbs-up gesture conveys enthusiasm and positive engagement. The Apple iMac and informal bedroom/study setting reinforce the 'run by high school and college students' brand narrative.

nodes.label: Mittler Senior Technology Website After Redesign
nodes.description: EXTRACTED: A full-page screenshot of the redesigned Mittler Senior Technology website. The hero section features bold white headline text 'Technology Education for Senior Citizens' over a blurred background image (the hero-bg photo of a young tutor at a computer). Below the headline is a short tagline and two prominent CTA buttons labeled 'For Seniors' and 'For Organizations' in green. Below the hero are four stat tiles showing '3,500+ Seniors Impacted', '450+ Classes Taught', '150+ Teens Inspired', '10+ Countries Reached'. A 'Classes & Services' section heading appears below. The navigation bar is clean with white background, green logo, and right-aligned nav links including a 'Sponsor a Class' CTA button in green. INFERRED: The redesign emphasizes social proof, clarity, and audience segmentation (seniors vs. organizations). The green color palette aligns with the existing brand. The layout is modern, spacious, and conversion-focused with statistics driving credibility.
nodes.visual_tone: modern, clean, credibility-focused, green brand palette

nodes.label: Mittler Senior Technology Website Before Redesign
nodes.description: EXTRACTED: A full-page screenshot of the original Mittler Senior Technology website before redesign. The top section contains a dark navy banner with large bold yellow text reading 'TECH TIP TUESDAY' alongside white body text describing a weekly tech tip clip series distributed via WhatsApp, email, YouTube, and social media. Below is a two-column layout with sections titled 'Private Technology Assistance' and 'Group Classes', both using dense paragraph text with 'click here' hyperlinks. A left sidebar contains navigation links (Home, Classes & Services, Our Story, Our Mission, Contact & Tutorials, Testimonials, In The News, Sample Classes). Bottom row shows three small thumbnail images. The overall visual design is outdated, text-heavy, low contrast, and lacks a clear visual hierarchy. INFERRED: The old site predates modern UI conventions, with no prominent hero, no stat-driven social proof, no audience segmentation, and an overloaded navigation structure. The 'click here' anchor text is a known accessibility anti-pattern.
nodes.visual_tone: dated, cluttered, low visual hierarchy, text-heavy

nodes.label: Nature Sustainability Case Study Hero
nodes.description: EXTRACTED: A wide-angle photograph of a solar panel array (photovoltaic field) extending diagonally across the lower half of the frame under a vivid bright blue sky with wispy white cirrus clouds. The panels are mounted on metal support structures and appear to be in an open rural or semi-arid landscape. INFERRED: This image is used as the hero/banner background for a sustainability or clean energy case study. The expansive sky and solar panels visually communicate themes of renewable energy, environmental sustainability, clean technology, and large-scale impact. The bright blue and silver tones project optimism and forward-thinking.

nodes.label: Mittler Senior Tech Before/After Redesign Comparison
nodes.description: INFERRED: The before/after pair documents a significant UX and visual redesign. Before: sidebar-nav, dark announcement banner, dense prose, no social proof, low hierarchy, accessibility issues. After: sticky top nav, full-bleed hero with photo overlay, dual audience CTAs, stat tiles for credibility, modern spacing and typography. The redesign dramatically improves conversion potential and accessibility compliance.

edges.note: The hero-bg photo is visibly used as the blurred background image in the after-redesign hero section
Direct before/after pair documenting the same website pre- and post-redesign
Both are case study assets in the same portfolio; both use full-bleed photography as hero backgrounds for mission-driven organizations
Same brand/organization (Mittler Senior Technology); hero photo connects to both design states

hyperedges.label: Mittler Senior Technology Case Study Asset Group
hyperedges.note: All three image files plus derived comparison concept together constitute the Mittler Senior Technology website redesign case study
